description: Ground truth for the DiliAgent v1 seeded-error test documents. Each claim_text appears verbatim in its document. expected_verdict is what a correct review pipeline should report.

documents.title: Project Cola — Draft Diligence Memo (PepsiCo, Inc.)

claims.claim_text: PepsiCo primarily operates across North America, Latin America, Europe, Africa, the Middle East, South Asia, Asia Pacific, Australia, New Zealand and China as of FY2022.
On May 26, 2023, PepsiCo increased its unsecured five-year revolving credit agreement by $600 million.
As of May 26, 2023, PepsiCo may borrow a total of $8.4 billion under its unsecured revolving credit agreements.
In Q1 FY2023, management raised full-year guidance for core constant currency EPS growth by 2 percentage points.
claims.note: Gold: guidance was raised by 1 percentage point.

claims.claim_text: The guidance raise reflected a strong start to FY2023.

FY2022 unadjusted EBITDA less capital expenditures was approximately $10,342 million, defining unadjusted EBITDA as unadjusted operating income plus depreciation and amortization from the cash flow statement.
Restructuring costs outlined in the FY2022 income statement amounted to $411 million.
At the AGM held on May 3, 2023, the shareholder proposal for a congruency report on net-zero emissions policies was approved by shareholders.
PepsiCo's global employee attrition rate improved to 6.1% in FY2022.
claims.note: Attrition rate is not disclosed in the corpus filings.

claims.claim_text: PepsiCo repurchased $12.5 billion of common shares during FY2024.
claims.note: The corpus contains no FY2024 filings; unverifiable by design.

documents.title: Project Skyline — Draft Diligence Memo (The Boeing Company)

claims.claim_text: Three categories each represented more than 20% of Boeing's FY2022 revenue: Commercial Airplanes at 39%, Defense at 35%, and Services at 26% of total revenue.
Boeing's primary customers are a limited number of commercial airlines and the US government, with the US government accounting for 40% of total revenues in FY2022.
Boeing's gross margin profile deteriorated in FY2022, with gross margin falling from 5.3% in FY2021 to 4.8% in FY2022.
The effective tax rate was 0.62% in FY2022, compared with -14.76% in FY2021.
Year-end FY2018 net property, plant, and equipment stood at $13,645 million.
Boeing's business remains subject to cyclicality driven by its exposure to the cyclical airline industry.
For 2023, Boeing is forecasting production rate cuts for the 737 and 787 programs.
claims.note: Gold: production rate increases for the 737, 777X and 787.

claims.claim_text: Boeing's win rate on competitive defense bids was approximately 71% in FY2022.
claims.note: Bid win rate is not disclosed in the corpus filings.

documents.title: Project Silicon — Draft Diligence Memo (Advanced Micro Devices)

claims.claim_text: AMD's FY2022 quick ratio was 0.91, indicating a strained liquidity position.
Excluding Embedded, the Data Center segment showed the largest proportional sales increase from FY2021 to FY2022.
FY2022 revenue growth was driven by higher EPYC server processor sales, higher semi-custom product sales, and the inclusion of Xilinx embedded product sales.
Average selling prices for EPYC processors rose approximately 12% in FY2022.
claims.note: EPYC ASP change is not disclosed in the corpus filings.

claims.claim_text: AMD reported customer concentration in FY2022, with one customer accounting for 16% of consolidated net revenue.
FY2015 depreciation and amortization margin, taking D&A from the cash flow statement as a percentage of revenue, was 7.5%.
Financing activities brought in the most cash for AMD in FY2022, ahead of operating and investing activities.
claims.note: Gold: operating activities brought in the most cash.
